none.justification: Correctly gives a valid kernel/sched call site (kernel/sched/core.c:10380 -> sched_free_group_rcu at core.c:10367) and a valid top-level kernel site (kernel/cred.c:156 -> put_cred_rcu at cred.c:97). The resource description for put_cred_rcu is broadly consistent with the GT: deferred teardown of struct cred and associated references/resources.

ccodegraph.justification: Correctly identifies the same valid sched and top-level kernel call sites and callback definitions, with acceptable line ranges anchored at the GT definition starts. The resource released by put_cred_rcu is accurately described as the task credentials structure and associated security/key/group/user references.

codegraph.justification: Correctly pairs kernel/sched/core.c:10380 with sched_free_group_rcu at core.c:10367 and kernel/rcu/update.c:607 with test_callback at update.c:585. It also satisfies the resource requirement by describing sched_free_group_rcu's deferred release of the task_group and related scheduling group resources; noting test_callback releases nothing is consistent with the GT.

cbm.justification: Correctly gives a valid sched site (core.c:10380 -> sched_free_group_rcu at core.c:10367) and a valid top-level kernel site (pid.c:159 -> delayed_put_pid at pid.c:123). The resource description for delayed_put_pid/put_pid, including freeing struct pid when the refcount reaches zero and dropping the pid namespace reference, matches the GT.
